gene_full_name: Major Histocompatibility Complex, Class II, DQ Alpha 1
region: Regulatory region, tag SNP for HLA-DQ2.5
description: HLA-DQA1 rs2187668 tag SNP for HLA-DQ2.5 heterodimer (DQA1*05 and DQB1*02 on same chromosome). The A allele (inferred DQ2.5) is necessary for celiac disease pathogenesis, present in ~95% of celiac patients vs 30% of general population.

source.title: A genome-wide association study for celiac disease identifies risk variants in the region harboring IL2 and IL21
source.authors: van Heel DA, Hunt K, Greco L, Dubois PC, et al.

TT.phenotype: High genetic predisposition to celiac disease; HLA-DQ2.5 homozygous
TT.effect: High genetic predisposition to celiac disease; HLA-DQ2.5 homozygous
TT.recommendation: Screen with tissue transglutaminase (tTG-IgA) serology. If positive, confirmed with endoscopic small bowel biopsy. Strict gluten-free diet if celiac disease confirmed. Monitor for nutritional deficiencies. Genetic counseling for family members. Screen for dermatitis herpetiformis, thyroiditis, type 1 diabetes.

TC.phenotype: Moderate genetic predisposition to celiac disease; HLA-DQ2.5 heterozygous
TC.effect: Moderate genetic predisposition to celiac disease; HLA-DQ2.5 heterozygous
TC.recommendation: Screen for celiac disease with tTG-IgA serology if symptoms suggestive. Follow-up with biopsy if serology positive. Strict gluten-free diet if diagnosed. Monitor for associated autoimmune conditions.

CT.phenotype: Moderate genetic predisposition to celiac disease; HLA-DQ2.5 heterozygous
CT.effect: Moderate genetic predisposition to celiac disease; HLA-DQ2.5 heterozygous
CT.recommendation: Screen for celiac disease with tTG-IgA serology if symptoms suggestive. Follow-up with biopsy if serology positive. Strict gluten-free diet if diagnosed. Monitor for associated autoimmune conditions.

CC.phenotype: Very low genetic risk for celiac disease; no HLA-DQ2.5
CC.effect: Very low genetic risk for celiac disease; no HLA-DQ2.5
CC.recommendation: Celiac disease unlikely if HLA-DQ2 and DQ8 both negative. Standard evaluation if celiac symptoms present despite negative HLA.
